methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $29.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

data.narrative:
Anysphere is a Series D AI & Software company founded by Michael Truell and Sualeh Asif. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private companies (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, Hive AI, and others) and one public peer (Palantir, PLTR). Anysphere has raised $3.4B+ to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $29.3B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.5x multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yields $44.0B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables—Wayve ($8.6B), Cohere ($7.0B), Hugging Face ($4.5B), and Hive AI ($2.0B)—produces a median of $5.8B, with a range of $2.0B to $8.6B. These divergent results reflect material tension between the company's last-round valuation and peer trading multiples.

The triangulated valuation range is $4.9B (low) to $50.5B (high), with a median of $24.9B. The wide spread underscores key uncertainties: whether Anysphere's current $29.3B post-money reflects founder conviction, investor scarcity premium, or genuine revenue/moat differentiation relative to the peer set. The peer-median method suggests substantial mark-down risk if benchmarked against recent AI funding activity, while stage-progression supports modest appreciation. Resolution depends on revenue trajectory and competitive positioning vis-à-vis named peers.
